questions.text: Arrange the following stages in the correct chronological order for a standard DAO proposal lifecycle using OpenZeppelin Governor + Timelock.
Which of the following are parameters you can configure in an OpenZeppelin Governor contract? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Quorum (as a fraction of total supply).
Proposal threshold (minimum tokens to create a proposal).
The exact code to be executed on each target contract.

questions.explanation: The Governor contract's parameters are set in the contract itself (delay, period, quorum fraction, threshold). The executable code is part of the proposal, not a contract parameter.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A, B, C, D

The Governor contract's parameters are set in the contract itself (delay, period, quorum fraction, threshold). The executable code is part of the proposal, not a contract parameter.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the timelock delay in a DAO proposal lifecycle?

options.text: To give token holders time to exit (sell) before a proposal takes effect, and to allow for final code review.
To prevent anyone from voting on the proposal.

questions.explanation: The timelock is a mandatory waiting period that protects the DAO by ensuring stakeholders have time to react before an approved proposal becomes irreversible.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

The timelock is a mandatory waiting period that protects the DAO by ensuring stakeholders have time to react before an approved proposal becomes irreversible.

questions.text: Why are MST's sub-paisa fees particularly advantageous for DAO proposal lifecycles?

options.text: They allow every stage - proposal creation, voting, queuing, and execution - to be affordable for all token holders, encouraging broad participation.

questions.explanation: On high-fee chains, only whales can afford to interact with the governance lifecycle. MST's low fees democratize every step of the process.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

On high-fee chains, only whales can afford to interact with the governance lifecycle. MST's low fees democratize every step of the process.
